policy.description: Blocking line-coverage ratchets. Update measured counts in the same reviewed change whenever scope or coverage advances; never lower a floor without an explicit, justified scope change.

tolerance.rationale: Two clean local Node 22/V8 runs on 2026-07-16 each passed 959 tests and measured the identical 1762-line core scope at 1438 covered lines after the independently reviewed issue #130 correction. The reviewed high-water is unchanged: routing tag-renderer storage through the fault-containing browser-storage adapter added three executable core lines, two covered and one recovery branch outside the existing tag-renderer tests; the follow-up registered-activation, canonical-integer, and pre-auth-diagnostic fixes live in the classic loader and test harness and therefore do not alter this core scope. The existing one-line tolerance is unchanged and permits only negligible instrumentation variance.

Two clean local .NET 10.0.9/Coverlet integration runs on 2026-07-16 each passed 1563 tests and measured the identical 26188-line assembly scope at 17593 and 17592 covered lines; the reviewed high-water is 17593. Issue #73 moves first-read settings initialization and conditional POST mutation behind one configuration-store-owned per-user/per-file transaction: the classified missing read, one live plugin-configuration snapshot, default construction, validation, revision check, and crash-safe atomic save can no longer interleave as an unlocked logical lost update. Deterministic store-boundary barriers cover GET observing missing while POST queues and later wins durably, POST committing before GET, two concurrent first GETs constructing exactly one initial value, and atomic persistence failure returning 503 without false initialization or temp residue before a clean retry. Existing authorization, strong-revision compatibility, corrupt-store quarantine, unknown-field round trips, and atomic-write tests remain green. The existing two-line tolerance is unchanged and covers only the reproduced one-line Coverlet instrumentation variance between otherwise identical clean runs (about 0.0038 percentage points).
